title: Getting Started with shinybrain
text: analysis result contains project metadata plus internal tibbles used parser graph builder: project files contexts symbols references nodes edges issues
code: library(shinybrain)  app_dir <- system.file("examples", "basic_app", package = "shinybrain") if (app_dir == "") app_dir <- file.path("inst", "examples", "basic_app") if (!dir.exists(app_dir)) app_dir <- file.path("examples", "basic_app")  result <- analyze_shiny_project(app_dir) names(result) #> [1] "project"    "files"      "contexts"   "symbols"    "references" #> [6] "nodes"      "edges"      "issues"

title: Workflows and Included Examples
text: happy-path example. includes: reactive() eventReactive() observeEvent() observe() reactiveVal() reactiveValues() renderText() renderTable() renderPlot() sourced helper functions side-effect calls write.csv() message()
code: library(shinybrain)  basic_app <- system.file("examples", "basic_app", package = "shinybrain") if (basic_app == "") basic_app <- file.path("inst", "examples", "basic_app") if (!dir.exists(basic_app)) basic_app <- file.path("examples", "basic_app")  basic_result <- analyze_shiny_project(basic_app) table(basic_result$contexts$context_type) #> < table of extent 0 >

title: Workflows and Included Examples
text: app designed analyzer edge cases rather runtime execution. includes: resolved source("helpers.R") missing source target dynamic source() call dynamic input[[...]] lookup
code: edge_app <- system.file("examples", "edge_case_app", package = "shinybrain") if (edge_app == "") edge_app <- file.path("inst", "examples", "edge_case_app") if (!dir.exists(edge_app)) edge_app <- file.path("examples", "edge_case_app")  edge_result <- analyze_shiny_project(edge_app) edge_result$issues[, c("severity", "issue_type")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   severity issue_type   #>   <chr>    <chr>        #> 1 error    missing_file

title: NA
text: Think acting. Read existing files writing code. concise output thorough reasoning. Prefer editing rewriting whole files. re-read files already read unless file may changed. Test code declaring code. sycophantic openers closing fluff. Keep solutions simple direct. User instructions always override file. use em-dashes anywhere Avoid mojibake Create pain-point.md file record challenges facing. Always commit unused files final project .ignore “.” ignore files.

title: shinybrain
text: shinybrain statically analyzes Shiny applications without running . parses app source code, identifies reactive contexts helper functions, resolves references across sourced files, builds portable “App Brain” can exported structured JSON, Markdown, HTML dependency report.

title: shinybrain
text: project-level parse Shiny code without launching app Reactive graph nodes edges inputs, reactives, outputs, observers, helpers, state Detection sourced files, missing source() targets, dynamic patterns resolved statically Export formats can hand humans, CI jobs, LLM workflows

code: library(shinybrain)  app_dir <- system.file("examples", "basic_app", package = "shinybrain") if (app_dir == "") app_dir <- file.path("inst", "examples", "basic_app") if (!dir.exists(app_dir)) app_dir <- file.path("examples", "basic_app")  result <- analyze_shiny_project(app_dir) brain  <- build_brain(result)  brain$summary print_brain_console(brain) export_brain_json(brain, "brain.json") export_brain_markdown(brain, "brain.md") export_brain_html(brain, "brain.html", open = FALSE)

title: Pain points
text: Running log friction encountered working shinybrain project_hub.

title: Pain points
text: Root cause: doLogout JS handler cleared login cookie path=/ . Posit Connect app lives subpath (e.g. /content/123/), login package sets cookie scoped subpath. Clearing path=/ silently fails. Symptom: clicking Logout OK Pending Approval modal page reloads user still “logged ” according cookie, causing infinite approval loop. Fix (app.R ~line 3056): Replaced doLogout JS call shared helper _clearCookieAllPaths iterates every path segment window.location.pathname deletes cookie level. Also added separate clearLoginCookie handler without reloading (used account creation).

what: Account creation triggers logout loop on Posit Connect
title: Pain points
text: Root cause: new user submits Create Account form (ca_submit handler), login::login_server detects valid credentials DB fires USER$logged_in = TRUE user dismisses backup-codes modal. approval observer sees approved=0, forces USER$logged_in = FALSE, shows “Account Pending Approval” modal. Clicking OK calls doLogout, (cookie fix) failed clear cookie, causing infinite reload loop. successful account insert, set session$userData$just_created_account <- TRUE call clearLoginCookie silently clear stale cookie. top observeEvent(USER$logged_in), just_created_account TRUE, silently reset login state return without showing Pending Approval modal. flag consumed first use subsequent real login attempts work normally.

what: Files not to deploy to Posit Connect
title: Pain points
text: run.R LaunchApp.hta Windows local-launch scripts; exclude deploy manifest. entire /R/ directory (bundled portable R runtime) must pushed Connect. HUB_DATA_SRC_SP env var must set Connect (references Windows SharePoint path). HUB_DATA_PATH must set Connect persistent directory outside app bundle parquet files users.sqlite survive redeployments.

what: Windows-only code paths (low risk on Connect)
title: Pain points
text: open_pdf() utils.R uses shell.exec() guarded .Platform$OS.type == "windows", falls browseURL Linux. crash risk browseURL -op headless server. called normal server-side flow. to_file_uri() produces file:// URIs Windows UNC/drive paths. resolve users hitting remote server. Stored SOP paths Windows paths broken links.

title: Pain points
text: R installed current execution sandbox (R --version reports command found). verification test behaviour done static reading rather running devtools::test() R CMD check. Consequence: edits state read/write classification dynamically proven correct . must re-run user CI environment R available.

what: Cannot delete the brace-expansion cruft directory
title: Pain points
text: tests/{testthat,fixtures/ nested child {basic_single_file,observer_and_state,source_chain}}/ exist result mkdir command used braces shell expand . Attempts rm -rf python shutil.rmtree fail EPERM (Operation permitted) inner directory. Workaround: outer directory added .Rbuildignore R CMD build / R CMD check include tarball. user delete directory manually outside sandbox using native file manager shell sufficient permissions.

title: Pain points
text: package full #' @export roxygen metadata man/ empty devtools::document() never run. NAMESPACE written hand pass library(shinybrain) expose public API. R CMD check still warn undocumented code objects user runs devtools::document() locally.

title: Pain points
text: spec (fixture 2 shinybrain_build.md) wants rv(val) classified state_write rv() state_read. previous implementation left function_call punted “Sprint 2” note. pass adds argument-count detection parse tree separate two cases. implementation relies getParseData() shape may vary slightly across R versions; tests fail older R, revisit .call_arg_count().

title: Pain points
text: three tests/testthat/test_*.R files used magrittr pipe locate source files without declaring magrittr dependency. Replaced base-R helper looks <pkg_root>/R. helper runs functions already search path, installed-package test runs skip sourcing step.

what: Source files hidden inside R/ subdirectories
title: Pain points
text: Symptom: devtools::document() warned Objects listed exports, present namespace 29 31 exported symbols. analyze_shiny_file summarize_analysis loaded. Root cause: package laid R/brain/, R/graph/, R/io/, R/ir/, R/parse/, R/resolve/, R/utils/. R package loading sources .R files top level R/; subdirectories silently ignored. Every file inside subdirectories invisible package loader. Fix: Flattened layout. files moved R/ top level: brain.R, export.R, insights.R, report.R, graph.R, io.R, project.R, constructors.R, parse.R, resolve.R, helpers.R. Sandbox blocker: rmdir (now empty) R/<subdir>/ directories fails EPERM just like earlier cruft directory. Added .Rbuildignore rule excluded source tarball. User must delete empty directories manually host.

title: Pain points
text: Symptom: Skipping NAMESPACE: already exists generated roxygen2. Root cause: roxygen2 refuses overwrite NAMESPACE lacks magic header comment. Fix: Replaced first line NAMESPACE expected # Generated roxygen2: edit hand marker. Created R/shinybrain-package.R containing package-level @importFrom tags (tibble, rlang, utils, stats) regeneration preserves imports. Next devtools::document() now overwrite NAMESPACE cleanly produce 31 exports plus four imports.

title: Pain points
text: Running example runner examples/basic_app/ reading resulting HTML uncovered four concrete gaps. four now wired .

what: 1. message/print/cat were dropped before reaching insights
title: Pain points
text: Root cause: sat skip_calls inside .extract_refs_for_context, reference ever emitted, side-effect insight layer nothing flag. Fix: moved skip_calls SIDE_EFFECT_FNS flow function_call references recognized side effects.

what: 2. reactiveValues $ reads and $<- writes produced no edges
title: Pain points
text: Root cause: parser emitted references input$x / output$y dollar access. obj$key read silently dropped, counters$clicks similar edge state node. Fix: $ handler .extract_refs_for_context now walks enclosing assignment expr detect whether dollar access LHS <- / = / ->. LHS becomes state_write, otherwise state_read. resolve_references verifies base name known state symbol (matches state_values state_val), otherwise marks not_a_state_object edge builder skips .

what: 3. Missing sourced files were silently dropped from the graph
title: Pain points
text: Root cause: resolve_source_chain bailed source() target exist, user saw nothing nodes table broken link. Fix: missing paths now appended state$ordered_paths role = "missing" severity = "warning" issue emitted. build_nodes accepts files argument emits missing_file ghost node missing path. .vis_nodes renders dashed red box visually distinct. brain_options(strict_missing_sources = TRUE) elevates warnings errors CI gates. load_file suppresses second duplicate issue called role = "missing".

what: 4. saved(NULL) was classified as state_read instead of state_write
title: Pain points
text: Root cause: .count_call_args counted expr children parens. R’s parser always wrap literal constants (NULL_CONST, NUM_CONST, STR_CONST, TRUE, FALSE, NA) enclosing expr token, saved(NULL) sole argument appeared bare NULL_CONST count came back 0. resolve_references used 0 classify read. Fix: .count_call_args now counts top-level commas parens. Zero children returns 0; otherwise returns n_commas + 1. independent whether parser wraps literals expr. Diagnostic: added brain_options(verbose = TRUE). set, build_brain prints every state-like reference target_arg_count, resolved symbol, whether edge produced, future mystery drops surface immediately.

title: Pain points
text: Swept every rlang::abort make_issue call rewrote answer three questions: happened, (file + line), next. previous messages terse useful , e.g. "Dynamic source() path line 12 - skipped" say file line referred , “dynamic” path meant, fix . Concrete changes (R/ top-level sources): R/project.R: dynamic source() warnings now name calling file, explain runtime-path cause, suggest replacing bare string literal. Missing source() targets show raw path, resolved candidate, calling file + line. Circular source chains now print full .R -> b.R -> .R cycle suggest fix. catch-“Directory found” analyze_shiny_project() explains paths accepted points analyze_shiny_file() single-file use. R/io.R: detect_entry_point() now lists .R files present directory, flags ui.R-without-server.R (vice versa) near-miss, suggests analyze_shiny_file() subdirectory layout non-standard. load_file() surfaces underlying readLines() error (locked file, permissions, bad encoding) instead useless “read file” string. “File empty” notes informational completed successfully. R/parse.R: parse-failure messages prefixed file basename, include R’s parse error, show exact parse(file = "...") call reproduce outside shinybrain. Side-effect flag messages now explain shinybrain flagged call (effect represented graph edge) safe ignore. R/constructors.R: validate_ir() schema errors list valid schema names, columns expected, columns actually got, point new_sb_<schema>() starting point hand-built tibble. Type-mismatch errors suggest fix. R/graph.R: duplicate-node-id orphan-edge messages tagged “Internal” labelled shinybrain bugs request report . End users never see , now know ’s code. R/export.R: jsonlite missing-package error mentions export_brain_markdown() dependency-free alternative. touched files pass state-machine paren/brace balance check contain em-dashes. Messages kept plain ASCII render correctly CLI, HTML report, JSON export.

title: Brain export options — brain_options
text: include_snippets Logical. Include code snippets node data. snippet_max_lines Integer. Max lines per snippet. include_file_paths One "relative", "basename", "none". include_unresolved Logical. Include unresolved references export. strict_missing_sources Logical. TRUE, elevates missing-file warnings (unresolved source() targets) errors. ghost node still emitted either way. verbose Logical. TRUE, build_brain() prints diagnostic report every state-related reference (state_read, state_write, ambiguous state calls) target_arg_count. Useful state edge expected missing graph.

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: Concrete implementation guide: IR schemas, function signatures, fixture apps, Sprint 1 checklist.

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: contracts entire package builds toward. Every layer - parser, resolver, graph builder, exporter - reads writes structures. Lock writing functional code.

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: One row. Top-level project container.
code: sb_project <- tibble::tibble(   project_id       = character(),   # uuid, e.g. "proj_a1b2c3"   root_path        = character(),   # absolute path to project root   entry_point_type = character(),   # "app_r" | "ui_server_pair" | "single_file"   entry_files      = list(),        # character vector of entry file paths   parse_order      = list(),        # character vector: order files were parsed   shinybrain_version = character(), # package version that produced this   created_at       = character()    # ISO timestamp )

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: One row per source file parsed.
code: sb_file <- tibble::tibble(   file_id       = character(),  # "file_001"   project_id    = character(),  # foreign key to sb_project   path          = character(),  # absolute path   relative_path = character(),  # relative to project root   role          = character(),  # "entry" | "sourced" | "module"   source_line   = integer(),    # line in parent file where source() was called, NA if entry   code          = character(),  # full raw code as single string   line_count    = integer(),   parse_success = logical() )

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: One row per meaningful execution block. central IR object - everything can become graph node starts . context_type enum: Reserved future use: "module_def", "module_instance" - parser yet detect Shiny modules. module_id field schemas placeholder.
code: sb_context <- tibble::tibble(   context_id        = character(),  # "ctx_001"   project_id        = character(),   file_id           = character(),   context_type      = character(),  # see type enum below   label             = character(),  # e.g. "filtered_data", "output$plot", "observe_001"   qualified_name    = character(),  # "app.R::filtered_data"   line_start        = integer(),   line_end          = integer(),   parent_context_id = character(),  # NA if top-level; used for nested/module contexts   module_id         = character(),  # NA if not inside a module   snippet           = character(),  # extracted code body, trimmed to N lines   contains_isolate  = logical(),   confidence        = character(),  # "high" | "medium" | "low"   flags             = list()        # character vector of flags e.g. "possible_side_effect" ) "reactive" "output_render" "observer" "observe_event" "event_reactive" "helper_fn" "state_val"        # reactiveVal() "state_values"     # reactiveValues() "ui_block" "global_block" "unknown"

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: One row per named entity defined project. symbol_type enum: Reserved future use: "module_def", "module_instance"
code: sb_symbol <- tibble::tibble(   symbol_id          = character(),  # "sym_001"   project_id         = character(),   file_id            = character(),   context_id         = character(),  # context where this symbol is defined   name               = character(),  # e.g. "filtered_data", "clean_data", "rv"   qualified_name     = character(),  # "app.R::filtered_data"   symbol_type        = character(),  # see type enum below   line_start         = integer(),   line_end           = integer(),   module_id          = character(),  # NA if not module-scoped   usage_count        = integer(),    # how many contexts reference this symbol   confidence         = character() ) "input_ref"        # input$x - always read-only, never defined "output"           # output$x <- render* "reactive" "event_reactive" "observer" "observe_event" "helper_fn" "state_val" "state_values" "unresolved"

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: One row per reference one context symbol input. raw material graph edges. reference_type enum:
code: sb_reference <- tibble::tibble(   reference_id       = character(),  # "ref_001"   project_id         = character(),   from_context_id    = character(),  # context containing the reference   reference_type     = character(),  # see type enum below   target_text        = character(),  # raw text as written, e.g. "input$year", "clean_data"   resolved_symbol_id = character(),  # NA if unresolved   line_start         = integer(),   line_end           = integer(),   is_isolated        = logical(),    # TRUE if inside isolate()   is_dynamic         = logical(),    # TRUE if target cannot be statically determined   confidence         = character(),   unresolved_reason  = character()   # NA or unresolved type string (see section 3) ) "input_read" "reactive_read" "output_write" "state_read" "state_write" "function_call" "event_trigger" "namespace_use" "source_call" "module_instantiation" "ui_update_call"

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: Promoted sb_context resolution. One row per graph node. Renderer consumes , raw contexts.
code: sb_node_candidate <- tibble::tibble(   node_id           = character(),  # "node_001" - stable across renders   project_id        = character(),   context_id        = character(),  # FK to sb_context   node_type         = character(),  # final graph type (maps from context_type)   label             = character(),   qualified_name    = character(),   file_id           = character(),   line_start        = integer(),   module_id         = character(),   confidence        = character(),   contains_isolate  = logical(),   usage_count       = integer(),    # for helper_fn nodes only   flags             = list(),   snippet           = character() )

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: One row per directed relationship graph. edge_type enum:
code: sb_edge_candidate <- tibble::tibble(   edge_id      = character(),  # "edge_001"   project_id   = character(),   from_node_id = character(),   to_node_id   = character(),   edge_type    = character(),  # see type enum below   reference_id = character(),  # FK to sb_reference if applicable   is_isolated  = logical(),   confidence   = character(),   file_id      = character(),   line_start   = integer(),   flags        = list() ) "depends_on" "feeds_into" "triggers" "calls" "reads_state" "writes_state" "contains" "cross_module" "defines" "unresolved_link"

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: One row per warning, error, flagged pattern.
code: sb_issue <- tibble::tibble(   issue_id     = character(),  # "issue_001"   project_id   = character(),   file_id      = character(),  # NA if project-level   context_id   = character(),  # NA if not context-specific   severity     = character(),  # "info" | "warning" | "error"   issue_type   = character(),  # see unresolved taxonomy (section 3)   message      = character(),   line_start   = integer(),   line_end     = integer() )

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: Split internal pipeline functions user-facing functions. Build internal first. User-facing functions thin wrappers.

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
code: # Detect project entry point from a directory path # Returns list(type, files) or throws with guidance detect_entry_point(path)  # Load a single file - reads code, validates it exists # Returns sb_file row (unpopulated parse fields) load_file(path, project_id, role = "entry", source_line = NA_integer_)  # Resolve all source() calls recursively from an entry file # Returns character vector of file paths in parse order # Detects and stops on cycles, returns issue rows for cycles found resolve_source_chain(entry_path, project_root)

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
code: # Parse a single file - runs all 5 passes # Returns list(file = sb_file, contexts = sb_context, symbols = sb_symbol, references = sb_reference, issues = sb_issue) parse_file(sb_file_row)  # Pass 1: run parse() + getParseData(), attach to file object parse_raw(sb_file_row)  # Pass 2: scan top-level expressions # Returns list of typed expression records scan_top_level(parse_data, file_id)  # Pass 3: extract context objects from recognized call patterns # Returns sb_context tibble rows extract_contexts(top_level_exprs, file_id, project_id)  # Pass 4: extract intra-context references # Returns sb_reference tibble rows extract_references(contexts, parse_data, file_id, project_id)  # Pass 5: extract snippet for each context (trimmed to max_lines) extract_snippet(context_row, code, max_lines = 20)

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: yet implemented: resolve_modules() - module namespace resolution planned requires dedicated parser pass. module_id field reserved IR schemas purpose.
code: # Link sb_reference rows to sb_symbol rows where possible # Mutates resolved_symbol_id and updates confidence # Returns updated sb_reference tibble resolve_references(references, symbols, project_id)  # Check a symbol name against defined symbols # Returns symbol_id or NA with unresolved_reason lookup_symbol(name, symbols, from_context_id)

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
code: # Promote resolved contexts to node candidates # Returns sb_node_candidate tibble build_nodes(contexts, symbols, project_id)  # Build edge candidates from resolved references # Returns sb_edge_candidate tibble build_edges(references, nodes, symbols, project_id)  # Calculate usage count for helper_fn nodes # Mutates usage_count on sb_node_candidate compute_usage_weights(nodes, edges)  # Validate graph: check for orphan edges, duplicate IDs, etc. # Returns sb_issue rows for any graph-level problems validate_graph(nodes, edges, project_id)

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: Every unresolved_reason value must one strings. Used sb_reference, sb_issue, App Brain exports.
code: UNRESOLVED_TYPES <- c(   "dynamic_input_id",        # input[[paste0(...)]]   "dynamic_output_id",       # output[[name]] <- ...   "dynamic_source_path",     # source(file.path(...))   "runtime_generated_ui",    # renderUI internals   "conditional_definition",  # reactive inside if() block   "ambiguous_symbol",        # name matches multiple definitions   "unknown_callee",          # function call target not found in project   "nonstandard_evaluation",  # NSE patterns obscuring symbols   "unsupported_pattern",     # known limitation, not yet supported   "parse_failure",           # file could not be parsed at all   "module_link_incomplete",  # module wiring partially resolved   "source_cycle"             # circular source() chain detected )

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: Three reference apps Sprint 1. complete, runnable Shiny app. Store tests/fixtures/ package.

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: tests/fixtures/basic_single_file/app.R Expected parse results:
code: library(shiny)  # Simple helper function clean_data <- function(df) {   df[complete.cases(df), ] }  ui <- fluidPage(   titlePanel("Basic App"),   sidebarLayout(     sidebarPanel(       sliderInput("year", "Year", min = 2000, max = 2023, value = 2010),       selectInput("region", "Region",                   choices = c("North", "South", "East", "West"))     ),     mainPanel(       plotOutput("trend_plot"),       tableOutput("summary_table"),       textOutput("record_count")     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {    filtered_data <- reactive({     df <- mtcars     df <- clean_data(df)     df[df$cyl >= input$year %% 4 + 4, ]   })    summary_stats <- reactive({     data.frame(       region = input$region,       mean_mpg = mean(filtered_data()$mpg)     )   })    output$trend_plot <- renderPlot({     plot(filtered_data()$mpg, main = input$region)   })    output$summary_table <- renderTable({     summary_stats()   })    output$record_count <- renderText({     paste("Records:", nrow(filtered_data()))   })  }  shinyApp(ui, server) # Contexts: 5 #   filtered_data  -> reactive #   summary_stats  -> reactive #   trend_plot     -> output_render #   summary_table  -> output_render #   record_count   -> output_render #   clean_data     -> helper_fn  # Symbols: 6 (above + input refs)  # References from filtered_data: #   input$year        -> input_read,  confidence = high #   clean_data()      -> function_call, confidence = high  # References from summary_stats: #   input$region      -> input_read,  confidence = high #   filtered_data()   -> reactive_read, confidence = high  # References from trend_plot: #   filtered_data()   -> reactive_read, confidence = high #   input$region      -> input_read, confidence = high  # References from summary_table: #   summary_stats()   -> reactive_read, confidence = high  # References from record_count: #   filtered_data()   -> reactive_read, confidence = high  # Issues: 0 # Unresolved: 0 # clean_data usage_count: 1

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: tests/fixtures/observer_and_state/app.R Expected parse results:
code: library(shiny)  ui <- fluidPage(   numericInput("threshold", "Threshold", value = 5),   actionButton("reset_btn", "Reset"),   actionButton("save_btn", "Save"),   textOutput("status"),   tableOutput("filtered_table") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {    # State node - should NOT be classified as input   rv <- reactiveVal(NULL)    filtered <- reactive({     req(input$threshold)     mtcars[mtcars$cyl >= input$threshold, ]   })    # observeEvent - triggered by button   observeEvent(input$reset_btn, {     rv(NULL)     updateNumericInput(session, "threshold", value = 5)   })    # observeEvent - save action with side effect   observeEvent(input$save_btn, {     data <- filtered()     rv(data)     write.csv(data, "output.csv")  # side effect - should be flagged   })    output$status <- renderText({     if (is.null(rv())) "No data saved." else paste("Saved", nrow(rv()), "rows.")   })    output$filtered_table <- renderTable({     filtered()   })  }  shinyApp(ui, server) # Contexts: 6 #   rv              -> state_val #   filtered        -> reactive #   observe_reset   -> observe_event #   observe_save    -> observe_event  (flags: possible_side_effect) #   status          -> output_render #   filtered_table  -> output_render  # State nodes: rv - symbol_type = "state_val" (NOT "input_ref")  # References in observe_save: #   filtered()    -> reactive_read #   rv(data)      -> state_write #   write.csv()   -> function_call, flag: possible_side_effect  # References in observe_reset: #   rv(NULL)             -> state_write #   updateNumericInput() -> ui_update_call  # References in status: #   rv()          -> state_read  # Issues: 1 #   severity: "info" #   issue_type: "unsupported_pattern"  (write.csv side effect flagged)

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: tests/fixtures/source_chain/app.R tests/fixtures/source_chain/helpers.R tests/fixtures/source_chain/data_utils.R Expected parse results:
code: library(shiny)  source("helpers.R") source("data_utils.R")  ui <- fluidPage(   selectInput("metric", "Metric", choices = c("mpg", "hp", "wt")),   plotOutput("metric_plot"),   tableOutput("metric_table") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {    processed <- reactive({     prepare_data(mtcars, input$metric)   })    output$metric_plot <- renderPlot({     plot_metric(processed(), input$metric)   })    output$metric_table <- renderTable({     format_table(processed())   })  }  shinyApp(ui, server) # Plot utility - used in 1 reactive context plot_metric <- function(df, metric) {   barplot(df[[metric]], main = paste("Distribution of", metric)) }  # Table utility - used in 1 reactive context format_table <- function(df) {   head(df, 10) } # Core data prep - used in 1 reactive context # Higher usage would increase visual weight prepare_data <- function(df, metric) {   df <- df[!is.na(df[[metric]]), ]   df[order(df[[metric]]), ] } # Files parsed: 3 (app.R, helpers.R, data_utils.R) # source() chain resolved: app.R -> helpers.R, app.R -> data_utils.R  # Symbols from sourced files carry source_file metadata: #   plot_metric   -> helper_fn, file_id = "file_002" (helpers.R) #   format_table  -> helper_fn, file_id = "file_002" (helpers.R) #   prepare_data  -> helper_fn, file_id = "file_003" (data_utils.R)  # Contexts: 3 (processed, metric_plot, metric_table) + 3 helper fns  # References: #   processed -> prepare_data()  -> function_call, resolved = TRUE #   processed -> input$metric    -> input_read #   metric_plot -> plot_metric() -> function_call, resolved = TRUE #   metric_plot -> processed()   -> reactive_read #   metric_plot -> input$metric  -> input_read #   metric_table -> format_table() -> function_call, resolved = TRUE #   metric_table -> processed()  -> reactive_read  # usage_count: #   prepare_data: 1 #   plot_metric:  1 #   format_table: 1  # Issues: 0 # Unresolved: 0

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: Goal: analyze_shiny_file("app.R") returns trusted internal model. viewer. modules. source(). D3.

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: usethis::create_package("shinybrain") Create directory structure: R/io/, R/parse/, R/ir/, R/resolve/, R/graph/, R/brain/, R/utils/ Create tests/fixtures/ 3 fixture apps Add dependencies DESCRIPTION: tibble, rlang, utils, stringr Set testthat usethis::use_testthat()

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: Write new_sb_project() - returns empty validated tibble Write new_sb_file() - returns empty validated tibble Write new_sb_context() - returns empty validated tibble Write new_sb_symbol() - returns empty validated tibble Write new_sb_reference() - returns empty validated tibble Write new_sb_node_candidate() - returns empty validated tibble Write new_sb_edge_candidate() - returns empty validated tibble Write new_sb_issue() - returns empty validated tibble Write validate_ir() - checks required columns types exist Test: constructor produces expected column names types

text: Write load_file(path, project_id, role, source_line) Test: loads file, populates sb_file row, handles missing file gracefully sb_issue

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: Write parse_raw() - wraps parse() + getParseData(); handles parse errors, returns issue failure Write scan_top_level() - identifies assignments, function defs, function calls, source() calls Test fixture 1: correct number top-level expressions found

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: Detect reactive({ }) assignments → context_type = "reactive" Detect output$x <- render*({ }) → context_type = "output_render", label = output ID Detect observe({ }) → context_type = "observer" Detect observeEvent(trigger, { }) → context_type = "observe_event" Detect eventReactive(trigger, { }) → context_type = "event_reactive" Detect named function definitions → context_type = "helper_fn" Detect reactiveVal() → context_type = "state_val" Detect reactiveValues() → context_type = "state_values" Populate line_start, line_end, label, qualified_name Test fixture 1: 6 contexts extracted correct types Test fixture 2: rv classified state_val, input

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: Extract input$x references → reference_type = "input_read" Extract output$x <- references → reference_type = "output_write" Extract named function calls present project → reference_type = "function_call" Extract reactive invocations name() → reference_type = "reactive_read" Extract rv() reads → reference_type = "state_read" Extract rv(val) writes → reference_type = "state_write" Detect isolate({ }) blocks → mark enclosed references is_isolated = TRUE Detect write.*, save*, db*, POST, httr calls → set flag possible_side_effect Detect update*Input() calls → reference_type = "ui_update_call" Test fixture 1: references extracted correct types targets Test fixture 2: write.csv flagged, rv(data) classified state_write

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: Write extract_snippet() - extracts lines line_start:line_end code, trims max_lines Test: snippet matches expected lines fixture context

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: Write resolve_references() - links target_text symbol_id match found sb_symbol Unmatched references get unresolved_reason taxonomy Test fixture 1: helper function calls resolve correct symbol IDs Test fixture 2: rv reads/writes resolve correctly

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: Write build_nodes() - promotes sb_context rows sb_node_candidate Write build_edges() - derives sb_edge_candidate resolved sb_reference Write compute_usage_weights() - calculates usage_count helper_fn nodes Write validate_graph() - checks orphan edges, duplicate node IDs Test fixture 1: correct node count (6), edge count, usage_count clean_data Test fixture 2: state node present, write/read edges present

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: Wire passes end end Return named list: list(project, files, contexts, symbols, references, nodes, edges, issues) Test 3 fixtures: summary counts match expected values Test: unsupported construct produces sb_issue row, crash

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: File found → informative error, crash Unparseable R code → parse_success = FALSE + sb_issue, rest pipeline continues Empty file → 0 contexts, 0 symbols, crash File library() calls → 0 contexts, 0 symbols, crash input[[paste0("x", )]] → unresolved_reason = "dynamic_input_id", crash

title: ShinyBrain - v0.1 Build Package
text: Given analyze_shiny_file("tests/fixtures/basic_single_file/app.R"): Returns list 8 named tibbles nrow(contexts) == 6 nrow(symbols) >= 6 output_render contexts non-NA label reactive contexts input$year input$region extracted references clean_data helper usage_count == 1 nrow(issues) == 0 unresolved references Test suite passes 0 failures End ShinyBrain v0.1 Build Package

title: shinybrain 0.1.0
text: Initial public release shinybrain. Added static analysis single-file multi-file Shiny projects. Added source-chain resolution, missing-source detection, ghost-node support. Added graph construction inputs, reactives, outputs, observers, helpers, state. Added JSON, Markdown, HTML App Brain exports. Added example apps, vignettes, pkgdown configuration, CRAN submission materials.
